name: Endeavour Silver Corp.

profile.description: Endeavour Silver Corp. is a silver mining company engaged in the acquisition, exploration, development, extraction, processing, refining, and reclamation of mineral properties in Mexico, Chile, Peru, and the United States. The company operates the Guanacevi and Bolanitos mines in Mexico, the Terronera mine (commissioned 2024), and the Kolpa mine in Peru acquired in 2025. Incorporated in 1981, headquartered in Vancouver, Canada.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Endeavour successfully brought the Terronera mine into commercial production in late 2024 (a multi-year, $200M+ development project) and completed the transformative acquisition of the Kolpa mine in Peru, doubling production capacity and driving a 78% YoY increase in silver-equivalent ounce production in Q1 2026 [1]. These represent significant operational expansions that redefined the company's scale. AI Deployment (0/2): No evidence of AI-powered products or AI-driven operational savings. Mining operations rely on conventional extraction and processing methods. Market Share (1/2): Endeavour is a mid-tier silver producer — meaningful player but not a category leader. Q1 2026 revenue of $209.7M (up 230% YoY) driven by Terronera and Kolpa ramp-up positions them as a growing force, though they remain far smaller than peers like Pan American Silver or First Majestic [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): 2026 guidance of 14.6-15.6M silver-equivalent ounces provides clear production visibility, and the Pitarrilla project represents future growth optionality [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Exploration spending and mine development investment are the mining equivalent of R&D; Endeavour maintains active exploration programs across its properties and committed significant capital to Terronera development [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Endeavour's stated values framework — ICARE (Integrity, Courage, Accountability, Respect, Excellence) — provides a clear cultural foundation, and the company articulates a purpose around responsible mining and creating value for all stakeholders [2][3]. The mission is clear and identifies what the company does and for whom, but doesn't articulate a deeply transformative societal vision beyond responsible resource extraction. Community Impact (2/2): Endeavour has formally reported on sustainability and community programs for 10+ years, with integrated community relations at operational mines in Mexico and Peru [3]. The company's commitment to local community development, including education, health, and infrastructure programs in mining-adjacent communities, represents genuine community investment aligned with their stated values. Employee Retention (1/2): Glassdoor reviews highlight a 4.1 culture/values rating and 4.4 senior management score, with employees describing a 'wonderful corporate culture' and 'socially responsible mining company' [2]. These are strong signals for the mining industry. Insider Buying (0/2): No evidence of meaningful net insider buying by CEO Dan Dickson or other executives in the last 12 months was found.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. D/E of 39.0% is well below the 70% target, reflecting conservative leverage with $251M debt against $241M cash — nearly net debt-free [1][4]. Forward P/E of 7.4 is exceptionally low, well under the 20 target, reflecting either value opportunity or market skepticism about sustainability of current earnings. Revenue growth YoY of 230% driven by Terronera and Kolpa mine additions is exceptional, and 3-year revenue CAGR of 30.5% is strong [1]. However, trailing ROE of -3.9% and profit margin of -3.5% reflect the historical losses before Terronera and Kolpa ramped up — Q1 2026 showed $64.9M net income and $83.8M operating earnings, suggesting a dramatic inflection [1]. The trailing metrics penalize a company in rapid transformation; forward metrics are far more favorable. PEG ratio unavailable.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Silver mining is a physical-world operation involving underground and open-pit extraction, mineral processing, and precious metals refining — activities that AI structurally cannot displace [1]. The business requires geological expertise, heavy equipment operation, regulatory compliance across multiple jurisdictions (Mexico, Peru, Chile), and community relations that are inherently human and physical. Furthermore, AI is a net demand driver for silver — every advanced semiconductor, data center connector, and high-performance GPU uses silver for contacts and solder joints, and the AI buildout is increasing industrial silver demand [2]. Analysts see zero AI substitution risk for mining operations. AI Opportunity (0/1): Endeavour does not generate revenue from AI products or services. The company is a pure-play silver/gold miner with no AI-related revenue streams.

infinite_game.summary: Endeavour Silver demonstrates several hallmarks of infinite game thinking under CEO Dan Dickson: a multi-decade commitment to responsible silver mining, genuine community investment in mining-adjacent communities, and the courage to invest through commodity cycles. The ICARE values framework and 10+ years of sustainability reporting show organizational consistency. However, the mining industry's inherent cyclicality and commodity-price dependence create structural tension with Sinek's infinite ideal — the company's fortunes are ultimately tied to silver prices, limiting the degree to which purpose alone can drive outcomes.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Endeavour's ICARE values framework and commitment to responsible mining provide a purpose beyond pure profit extraction [2][3]. However, the cause of 'responsible silver mining' is not the kind of transformative, future-oriented Just Cause Sinek describes — it doesn't articulate a vision of a future state so appealing that people sacrifice to advance it. The company exists to extract and sell silver responsibly, which is admirable but not deeply distinctive from peers like First Majestic or Pan American Silver.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Glassdoor ratings of 4.1 for culture/values and 4.4 for senior management are strong for the mining industry, and employee reviews describe a 'wonderful corporate culture' and socially responsible employer [2]. With 2,160 employees across operations in Mexico, Peru, and Vancouver, maintaining consistent trust is challenging but the signals are positive. CEO Dickson has been in place since 2021, and the leadership team shows stability.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Endeavour competes in a commodity market alongside larger rivals like Pan American Silver, First Majestic, and Hecla Mining. Management's language focuses on operational improvement and cost discipline (targeting $27-$28/oz AISC) rather than 'beating' specific competitors [1]. The company's strategic decisions — acquiring Kolpa to diversify geographically, developing Terronera for cost competitiveness — reflect learning from industry best practices rather than a zero-sum competitive mindset.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Endeavour has evolved from a small Mexican silver producer to a multi-country, multi-mine operation through the Terronera development and Kolpa acquisition [1]. The geographic diversification from Mexico-only to Mexico/Peru/Chile represents meaningful strategic adaptation. However, the core business model remains unchanged — find silver, mine silver, sell silver. The company has not fundamentally reinvented itself the way Sinek's canonical examples have.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Endeavour invested over $200M to develop the Terronera mine during a period of silver price uncertainty, and completed the Kolpa acquisition to diversify operations — both required significant capital commitment with uncertain payoff [1]. CEO Dickson's willingness to take on debt for transformative growth demonstrates some courage. However, these are standard mining industry growth moves rather than the kind of principled, costly decisions that Sinek would celebrate as courageous leadership.

investment_thesis: Endeavour Silver is in the midst of a dramatic transformation, with Q1 2026 delivering $209.7M revenue (up 230% YoY) and $64.9M net income (vs. a $32.9M loss in Q1 2025) driven by the Terronera mine reaching commercial production and the Kolpa mine acquisition [1]. At a forward P/E of 7.4x and $2.9B market cap, the stock is attractively valued relative to its growth trajectory — 2026 guidance of 14.6-15.6M silver-equivalent ounces at $27-28/oz AISC positions the company for $800M+ annual revenue if silver prices hold near $30+/oz. The balance sheet is clean with $241M cash against $251M debt, and operating cash flow of $84.7M TTM is inflecting sharply higher.
thesis_continued: The primary risk is commodity price dependence — Endeavour's profitability is a direct function of silver and gold prices, which are inherently volatile and outside management's control [1]. A sustained silver price decline below $25/oz would compress margins significantly given the $27-28/oz AISC guidance. The trailing financial metrics (negative ROE, negative profit margin) reflect the pre-transformation company and may mislead value screens, but the rapid ramp creates execution risk — integrating Kolpa while simultaneously scaling Terronera across three countries tests operational capacity for a company that had 2,160 employees and $218M revenue just 12 months ago [4]. Geopolitical risk in Mexico (mining regulatory uncertainty) and Peru (community opposition to mining) adds operational overhang.
key_catalysts: Full-year ramp of Terronera and Kolpa mines driving 2026 production guidance of 14.6-15.6M silver-equivalent ounces, potentially tripling 2024 production levels
Silver price strength above $30/oz driven by AI-related industrial demand growth (semiconductor contacts, data center components) and monetary policy easing expectations
Pitarrilla project advancement providing next-generation growth optionality beyond the current mine portfolio
key_risks: Commodity price dependence: silver price decline below $25/oz would compress margins against $27-28/oz AISC guidance, potentially returning the company to losses
Operational execution risk integrating Kolpa acquisition while simultaneously scaling Terronera to full capacity across Mexico, Peru, and Chile operations
Geopolitical and regulatory risk in Mexico (mining tax reform proposals) and Peru (community opposition and permitting uncertainty) that could disrupt operations
sources: Endeavour Silver Q1 2026 Earnings Release and Earnings Call Transcript — SEC Form 6-K, May 2026
Endeavour Silver Corporate Website, ICARE Values, Leadership Page, and Glassdoor Reviews, 2025-2026
Endeavour Silver Sustainability Report and ESG Governance Framework — edrsilver.com, 2025
